evals.prompt: Humanize this: I wanted to reach out regarding the prototype-creator evaluation framework I've been developing. It's important to note that the tool leverages Playwright-based persona walkthroughs to comprehensively assess prototypes against their Jira acceptance criteria. I'd be happy to schedule a meeting to delve into the technical details — the evaluation covers seven usability dimensions and produces a robust HTML report. I believe this could be truly transformative for our workflow. Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions.
evals.expected_output: A casual Slack message in Evan's voice with no dashes, no AI buzzwords, and natural abbreviations
assertions: Output contains zero em dashes or en dashes (no — or – characters)
Output does not contain any of: 'delve', 'leverage', 'robust', 'comprehensive', 'transformative', 'don't hesitate'
Output does not contain 'I wanted to reach out' or 'It's important to note'
Output sounds casual (contains at least some of: lowercase 'im'/'bc'/'rn', dropped apostrophes, or abbreviations)
Output preserves the core meaning: eval framework, Playwright walkthroughs, Jira ACs, HTML report
Output ends with a 'Changed:' summary noting what was fixed
Output is shorter than the input text

evals.prompt: Respond to this message from my manager: Hey Evan, wanted to check in on progress with the prototype evaluator. Any blockers? Also wondering if you've had a chance to look at the consistency checker integration we discussed last week.
evals.expected_output: A casual reply in Evan's voice addressing both questions
assertions: Output contains zero em dashes or en dashes
Output addresses both questions (prototype evaluator progress AND consistency checker)
Output sounds like a casual Slack reply, not a formal email
Output does not start with 'I hope this message finds you' or similar formal opener
Output is appropriately short for a Slack reply (not an essay)

evals.prompt: Make this sound like me: I would like to express my interest in contributing to the working group. I have extensive experience with evaluation frameworks and would be delighted to share my insights. Furthermore, I believe my background in user experience research — combined with my technical skills — would make me a valuable addition to the team.
evals.expected_output: Casual, humble expression of interest matching Evan's intern voice
assertions: Output contains zero em dashes or en dashes
Output does not contain 'would like to express', 'extensive experience', 'delighted', 'furthermore', 'valuable addition'
Output sounds humble and curious, not self-promoting
Output preserves the intent of wanting to join the working group
Tone matches an enthusiastic intern, not a senior executive
